data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-30T01:36:28.539Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating this .NET Framework code to .NET 8 and need to ensure all Thread.Abort
usage is addressed. Check this code and provide your Thread.Abort migration guidance.

```csharp
public class SimpleWorker
{
    public void RunBatch(List<WorkItem> items)
    {
        var threads = new List<Thread>();

        foreach (var item in items)
        {
            var t = new Thread(() => Process(item));
            t.Start();
            threads.Add(t);
        }

        foreach (var t in threads)
            t.Join();
    }

    private void Process(WorkItem item)
    {
        // simulate work
        Thread.Sleep(100);
        item.Result = ComputeResult(item.Input);
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about migrating .NET Framework code to .NET 8, specifically around `Thread.Abort`. Let me analyze the code they provided.

Looking at the code, it doesn't actually use `Thread.Abort` — it uses `Thread` with `Start`/`Join` pattern. The user wants guidance on ensuring all `Thread.Abort` usage is addressed, but this code doesn't have any.

However, the broader concern is valid: in .NET 8, `Thread.Abort()` throws `PlatformNotSupportedException`. The code as-is doesn't call `Thread.Abort`, but the pattern of raw `Thread` usage could be improved for .NET 8 by migrating to `Task`-based parallelism which supports cooperative cancellation via `CancellationToken`.


I should provide practical guidance on how to refactor this code to use modern async patterns instead of raw threads.
